name: explore_project
description: Quickly explore a project's structure: find source files (Python, Go, C, Rust, TypeScript, JavaScript), list top-level directories, count files by type, identify config files, and show key subdirectories. Auto-detects language (Python, Go, C, Rust, TypeScript, or JavaScript) based on file counts.
when_to_use: Use at the very beginning of a task to understand the project layout. Replaces multiple 'find', 'ls', and 'wc' calls. Supports Python, Go, C, Rust, TypeScript, and JavaScript projects out of the box. For C projects, shows both .c and .h source files together. For Rust projects, shows Cargo.toml/lock info. For TypeScript projects, shows package.json and tsconfig.json. For JavaScript projects, shows *.js/*.jsx source files and package.json config.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Primary language: 'py' (Python), 'go' (Go), 'c' (C), 'rs' (Rust), 'ts' (TypeScript), 'js' (JavaScript), or 'auto' (detect from project, default: auto)

parameters.name: max_files
parameters.description: Maximum number of source filenames to show (default: 50)

examples.expected: Project structure overview with detected language, source files, directory listing, file counts, config files, and key subdirectory listing.
Project overview treating /app as a Go project, showing up to 30 Go source files.
Project overview treating /app as a C project, showing up to 30 .c/.h source files with separate counts.
Project overview treating /app as a Rust project, showing .rs files, Cargo config, and src/ directory.
Project overview treating /app as a TypeScript project, showing .ts/.tsx files and package config.
Project overview treating /app as a JavaScript project, showing .js/.jsx files and package config.

cost_saving_rationale: Combines 5-10 separate bash commands (find, ls, wc, multiple greps for config) into a single script call, cutting round trips and reducing output volume by only showing essential info in one pass. Rust language support eliminates separate find commands for .rs files while also showing Cargo.toml/lock metadata. TypeScript support shows both .ts and .tsx source files alongside package.json config. JavaScript support shows .js/.jsx source files and package config for Node.js projects.
